disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known mild rheumatoid arthritis maintained on weekly methotrexate plus daily paracetamol; no recent medication changes
trigger_question_examples: What conditions do you have and what medications do you take?
Tell me about the arthritis and the weekly tablet — what is it and have there been any changes?
Are you on any regular medicines?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got mild rheumatoid arthritis. I take a tablet once a week for it — I think it's methotrexate — and some paracetamol most days. Nothing's changed recently.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Prolonged morning stiffness lasting hours during this flare
trigger_question_examples: How long does the stiffness last in the mornings?
When you wake up, how long before you loosen up?
Is there morning stiffness, and roughly how many minutes or hours does it go for?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Mornings are the worst — I'm stiff for hours, it takes most of the morning before I can move a bit more freely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Widespread allodynia — skin painful to light touch over shoulders and hips — plus pervasive cognitive fog and profound fatigue
trigger_question_examples: Is your skin painful or tender to a light touch anywhere?
Where is the tenderness worst, and does even light touch hurt?
Are you getting brain fog or unusual tiredness with this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — even a light touch on my shoulders and hips really hurts, more than the joints do. And my head is so foggy and I've got no energy at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negative — no single joint that is intensely red, hot and maximally swollen; the hand swelling is mild, symmetric and bilateral
trigger_question_examples: Is any one joint far worse than the others — bright red, very hot, and hugely swollen?
Do you have a single joint that has blown up on its own?
Are the hands equally affected on both sides, or is one joint standing out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's both hands the same and it's only a bit puffy — nothing has blown up bright red or become really hot on its own. It's more the all-over aching that's the problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negative — no high fever and no shaking chills/rigors (relevant given methotrexate immunosuppression)
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever or shaking chills?
Have you been able to check your temperature — any rigors or drenching sweats?
Because you're on methotrexate, I want to check — any signs of infection like fever or chills?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that I can tell — I checked and it was 37.3. No shaking chills or sweats.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negative — no new facial/malar rash, no pleuritic chest pain or breathlessness, no change in urine or ankle swelling (screens against a severe lupus/systemic flare)
trigger_question_examples: Any new rash on your face or cheeks, chest pain when breathing, or breathlessness?
Have you noticed frothy or reduced urine, or new swelling in your legs?
Any new rashes, chest pain, or changes to your waterworks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no new rash on my face, no chest pain or trouble breathing, and my waterworks and legs seem normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Poor oral intake today, bedbound, and lives alone — a dehydration and social-isolation deterioration risk
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to eat and drink today, and is there anyone with you?
Are you managing fluids, and do you have someone who can check on you?
Who is at home with you, and how are you going with drinking today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've barely had a drink today — I couldn't get up to the kitchen with the pain. And I live on my own, there's no one here.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: This flare pattern is familiar to the patient (recurrent widespread-pain-and-fatigue flares) but arrived faster and harder than usual
trigger_question_examples: Have you had episodes like this before — is this your usual flare or different?
Is this pattern familiar to you?
How does this compare to previous flares?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I do get flares of the all-over pain and exhaustion now and then, but this one came on faster and hit harder than they usually do, which is why I got worried.
